message:
fix(security): fail closed on key material mismatch during re-bind, enforce case-insensitive identity comparison in approval quorum, and harden capsule contract checks

Key role separation (key_role_separation.rs):
- Add KeyMaterialMismatch error variant so that re-binding the same
  key_id and role with different public key material fails closed
  instead of silently keeping the original material, preventing the
  caller from believing a new key is active when old material persists
- Refactor bind() to compare public_key_bytes on idempotent re-bind
  and return the new error when they differ
- Add KRS_KEY_MATERIAL_MISMATCH event code and Display implementation

Approval workflow (approval_workflow.rs):
- Fix quorum inflation vulnerability: a proposer signing with a
  different-case variant of their identity (e.g., "alice" vs "Alice")
  was counted as a non-proposer approval, potentially reaching quorum
  without genuine independent approvals
- Make required_approvers check case-insensitive, consistent with the
  sole-approver and dedup checks, so "bob" satisfies a "Bob"
  requirement
- Add regression tests for both the case-variant quorum inflation bug
  and the case-insensitive required_approvers matching

Verifier SDK capsule contract (check_verifier_sdk_capsule.py):
- Extract _required_checks_pass() helper that fails closed when any
  required check is missing from the results list, instead of the
  previous all() comprehension that would vacuously return True when
  a required check was absent
- Apply _required_checks_pass() to both
  workspace_sdk_structural_only_posture_explicit and
  connector_signature_authority_explicit contract assertions
- Add tests verifying the contract correctly fails closed when a
  required check is missing from the results

Beads issue tracker updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-21z","title":"[10.5] Implement signed decision receipt export for high-impact actions.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.5 — Security + Policy Product Surfaces (Item 2 of 8)

Why This Exists:
Signed decision receipts are the audit backbone of the policy system. Every high-impact action (quarantine, revocation, policy change, deployment promotion) must produce a cryptographically signed receipt that captures the decision context, evidence used, action taken, and rollback path. This enables deterministic post-hoc analysis and external verification.

Task Objective:
Implement signed decision receipt export for all high-impact actions in the policy/control system.

Detailed Acceptance Criteria:
1. Decision receipt schema captures: action type, decision timestamp, evidence references (ledger entry IDs), actor identity, policy rule chain that authorized the action, confidence context, and rollback command.
2. Receipts are cryptographically signed by the acting control-plane identity.
3. Receipt export in both machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable formats.
4. Receipts are append-only and hash-chained for tamper evidence.
5. CLI surface: receipt export accessible via franken-node incident bundle and trust commands.
6. Receipts integrate with evidence ledger (10.14) and operator copilot (10.0.8).
7. High-impact action classes requiring receipts: quarantine, revocation, policy change, deployment promotion, trust-level transition.

Key Dependencies:
- Depends on 10.14 (FrankenSQLite Deep-Mined) for evidence ledger integration.
- Depends on 10.13 (FCP Deep-Mined) for authenticated control channel.
- Consumed by 10.8 (Operational Readiness) for incident bundle retention.
- Consumed by 10.17 (Radical Expansion) for verifier SDK integration.

Expected Artifacts:
- src/security/decision_receipt.rs — receipt schema, signing, export.
- CLI integration for receipt export in incident and trust commands.
- docs/specs/section_10_5/bd-21z_contract.md
- artifacts/section_10_5/bd-21z/verification_evidence.json

Testing and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests: receipt construction, signing, hash chain integrity, schema validation.
- Integration tests: full action -> receipt generation -> export -> verification pipeline.
- E2E tests: franken-node incident bundle producing complete receipt chain.
- Adversarial tests: receipt tampering detection, replay of receipts from different contexts.
- Structured logs: DECISION_RECEIPT_GENERATED, RECEIPT_SIGNED, RECEIPT_EXPORTED, HASH_CHAIN_VERIFIED with trace IDs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define a SignedReceipt struct with fields: receipt_id (UUID v7), action_name (string), actor_identity (string), timestamp (RFC-3339), input_hash (SHA-256 of serialized action input), output_hash (SHA-256 of serialized action output), decision (enum: Approved | Denied | Escalated), rationale (string), and signature (Ed25519 detached signature, base64-encoded).
2. Implement sign_receipt(receipt: &Receipt, signing_key: &Ed25519PrivateKey) -> SignedReceipt that produces a deterministic canonical JSON serialization before signing (keys sorted, no optional whitespace).
3. Implement verify_receipt(signed: &SignedReceipt, public_key: &Ed25519PublicKey) -> Result<bool> that returns true only if the signature matches the canonical form.
4. High-impact actions are tagged via a #[high_impact] attribute macro or a runtime registry; any action so tagged must produce a receipt or the call returns Err.
5. Receipts must be exportable as both JSON and CBOR; round-trip fidelity test must pass (deserialize(serialize(r)) == r).
6. Provide an export_receipts(filter: ReceiptQuery) -> Vec<SignedReceipt> query API supporting time-range and action-name filters.
7. Verification: scripts/check_signed_receipt.py --json validates signature correctness on sample receipts; unit tests cover sign, verify, tamper-detection (flipped bit fails verify), and round-trip; evidence artifact in artifacts/section_10_5/bd-21z/.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyPond","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:46.140259819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T17:29:09.808505224Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T17:29:09.808475899Z","close_reason":"Completed: signed receipt schema/sign/verify/export + CLI/spec/tests/evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-5","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
 {"id":"bd-229","title":"[PLAN 10.3] Migration System","description":"Section: 10.3 — Migration System

Strategic Context:
Migration autopilot pipeline from audit to rollout, designed to collapse migration friction with deterministic confidence and replayability.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.3] Migration System" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff  {"id":"bd-22e7","title":"[5] Method Stack Compliance — 4 mandatory execution disciplines","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md — Section 5

## Why This Exists
franken_node is driven by four complementary methodologies. These are not optional preferences; they are mandatory execution disciplines. Every implementation bead must comply with at least one of these method stacks, and cross-cutting beads must demonstrate compliance with all applicable stacks.

## Four Required Method Stacks

### 5.1 extreme-software-optimization (Execution Discipline)
Mandatory loop for every performance-sensitive change:
1. Baseline (p50/p95/p99, throughput, memory, cold start)
2. Profile (top hotspots only)
3. Prove behavior invariance and compatibility envelopes
4. Implement one lever
5. Verify compatibility/security artifacts
6. Re-profile
NO optimization lands without artifact-backed regression safety.

### 5.2 alien-artifact-coding (Mathematical Decision Core)
Use formal decision systems for product control surfaces:
- Expected-loss rollout choices
- Posterior trust state updates
- Confidence-aware migration recommendations
- Explainable policy decisions and receipts

### 5.3 alien-graveyard (High-EV Primitive Selection)
Adopt only high-EV disruptive primitives with fallback contracts:
- EV thresholding (EV >= 2.0)
- Failure-mode predesign
- Deterministic degraded operation pathways

### 5.4 porting-to-rust (Spec-First Essence Extraction Protocol)
For compatibility surfaces, apply the porting discipline as extraction-and-proof:
- Extract behavior into explicit specs (data shapes, invariants, defaults, errors, edge cases)
- Capture Node/Bun fixture outputs as conformance baselines
- Implement from spec and fixture contracts, NOT legacy source structure
- Enforce parity and divergence visibility via lockstep oracle + artifact gates
RULE: Legacy code is input to specification and oracle generation, NOT implementation blueprint.

## Compliance Mapping to Execution Tracks
- 5.1 (perf discipline): Required for 10.6, 10.14, 10.15, 10.17, 10.18
- 5.2 (math decision): Required for 10.5, 10.17, 10.19, 10.20, 10.21
- 5.3 (high-EV primitives): Required for all 10.x tracks adopting advanced primitives
- 5.4 (spec-first extraction): Required for 10.2, 10.3, 10.7

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each implementation PR cites which method stack(s) it follows
- Performance PRs include before/after baseline artifacts (5.1)
- Decision-surface PRs include formal decision rationale (5.2)
- New primitive adoption PRs include EV analysis and fallback contract (5.3)
- Compatibility PRs include spec reference and fixture IDs (5.4)

## Testing Requirements
- CI gate checking for method stack citation in PR descriptions
- Audit script validating artifact presence for performance changes
- Review checklist enforcing method stack compliance


## Additional Verification Requirements
- Unit tests for method-stack compliance validators (citation checks, artifact-rule checks, stack-to-change classification).
- E2E compliance scripts that execute representative change flows and verify required method-stack evidence is produced end-to-end.
- Structured logs for compliance evaluations with deterministic rule IDs, pass/fail status, and remediation guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T16:14:34.097919725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T21:02:44.888350975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T21:02:44.888326650Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting","methodology","plan","section-5"]}

That leaves the Rust conformance fixture surface out of sync with the implementation and can break all-target verification. Update the conformance fixtures to supply deterministic replica IDs while preserving commutativity, associativity, and idempotency intent, then validate with rch-targeted cargo checks/tests only.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AzureCave","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:00:00.104768953Z","created_by":"AzureCave","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:00:20.786145292Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-22yy","title":"[10.14] Add DPOR-style schedule exploration gates for control/epoch/remote protocols.","description":"## Why This Exists

Dynamic Partial Order Reduction (DPOR) is a state-space exploration technique that systematically discovers concurrency bugs by exploring different interleavings of concurrent operations, but pruning equivalent schedules to keep the search tractable. For the 9J runtime, where epoch transitions, remote effects, and marker operations can interleave in complex ways, DPOR exploration is the most rigorous way to verify that no schedule produces a safety violation. Manual testing or random fuzzing cannot provide the coverage guarantees that DPOR offers. This bead directly enforces runtime invariant #9 (deterministic verification gates: protocol correctness is explored, not assumed) and feeds into the conformance suite (bd-3i6c). It targets specific protocol classes: epoch barrier coordination, remote capability operations, and marker stream mutations.

## What This Must Do

1. Implement a DPOR-style schedule explorer in `tests/lab/dpor_protocol_exploration.rs` that takes a protocol model (a set of concurrent operations with dependency annotations) and explores all non-equivalent schedules.
2. Define protocol models for: (a) epoch barrier coordination (propose/drain/commit across N participants), (b) remote capability operations (acquire/execute/release with concurrent epoch transitions), (c) marker stream mutations (concurrent append attempts with fencing).
3. For each model, define safety properties as assertions: (a) no two epochs are active simultaneously, (b) no remote operation executes without valid capability, (c) marker sequence is dense and hash-chain-valid after all operations complete.
4. On safety property violation, emit a minimal counterexample trace: the shortest schedule that triggers the violation, with exact operation ordering and intermediate states.
5. Implement bounded CI budget: the explorer must complete within a configurable time limit (default: 60 seconds per model) and report coverage metrics (explored schedules / estimated total schedules).
6. Produce a scope document defining which protocol classes are covered, what safety properties are checked, and what the coverage targets are.

## Acceptance Criteria

- DPOR explorer covers targeted protocol classes; minimal counterexample traces are emitted on failure; gate runs within bounded CI budget.
- Explorer completes epoch barrier model exploration within 60 seconds for N=3 participants.
- Explorer completes remote capability model exploration within 60 seconds.
- Explorer completes marker stream model exploration within 60 seconds.
- A deliberately buggy model (e.g., barrier that commits without full drain) produces a counterexample trace within 10 seconds.
- Counterexample trace includes exact operation ordering (step-by-step), intermediate states, and the violated safety property.
- Coverage report shows percentage of explored schedules and is included in verification evidence.
- Explorer does not exceed memory budget (configurable, default 1GB).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: exploration of a trivial 2-operation model (verify all interleavings explored); counterexample emission for known-buggy model; timeout enforcement; coverage metric accuracy.
- Integration tests: full exploration of epoch barrier model with 3 participants; full exploration of remote capability model; full exploration of marker stream model; deliberately buggy versions of each model produce counterexamples.
- Conformance tests: `tests/lab/dpor_protocol_exploration.rs` -- the DPOR gate tests.
- Structured logs: `DPOR_EXPLORATION_START` (model_name, estimated_schedules, budget_sec, trace_id), `DPOR_SCHEDULE_EXPLORED` (model_name, schedule_index, trace_id -- debug level), `DPOR_VIOLATION_FOUND` (model_name, property_name, counterexample_length, trace_id), `DPOR_EXPLORATION_COMPLETE` (model_name, explored_count, total_estimated, elapsed_sec, trace_id), `DPOR_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` (model_name, explored_count, budget_sec, trace_id).

## Expected Artifacts

- `tests/lab/dpor_protocol_exploration.rs` -- DPOR explorer and protocol model tests
- `docs/testing/dpor_gate_scope.md` -- scope document defining covered protocols and properties
- `artifacts/10.14/dpor_exploration_summary.json` -- exploration summary with coverage metrics
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-22yy/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-22yy/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-3hdv (monotonic control epoch -- epoch model depends on epoch semantics), bd-ac83 (named remote computation registry -- remote model depends on computation identifiers).
- Downstream: bd-3i6c (conformance suite -- uses DPOR results as evidence), bd-25oa (10.15 canonical DPOR gate), bd-3epz (section gate), bd-5rh (section gate).","acceptance_criteria":"DPOR explorer covers targeted protocol classes; minimal counterexample traces are emitted on failure; gate runs within bounded CI budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:59.312014395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:40:51.983527670Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:40:51.983470724Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22yy","depends_on_id":"bd-3hdv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T16:24:30.315869111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22yy","depends_on_id":"bd-ac83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T16:24:30.501713263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-232t","title":"[10.21] Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Why This Exists:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Task Objective:
Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Trust surfaces show "current state + trajectory path" with interpretable risk deltas; adversary posteriors account for evolution velocity and suspicious sequence motifs.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/bpet/trust_surface_integration.rs`, `tests/integration/bpet_trust_card_integration.rs`, `artifacts/10.21/bpet_trust_surface_snapshot.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-232t/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-232t/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.21] Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.21] Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.21] Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.21] Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.21] Integrate BPET trajectory signals into trust cards and adversary graph posterior updates." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Trust surfaces show "current state + trajectory path" with interpretable risk deltas; adversary posteriors account for evolution velocity and suspicious sequence motifs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:08.376285199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:04.707034784Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:04.707006802Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-21","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-232t","depends_on_id":"bd-1jpc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:05:47.831418490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-n9r","title":"Implement configuration system (franken_node.toml) with profile support","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md (Bootstrap bridge for Sections 10.0, 10.2, 10.3)
Section: BOOTSTRAP (Configuration baseline bridge)

Task Objective:
Implement the foundational `franken_node.toml` configuration system with profile support and deterministic precedence resolution for CLI, env, and file inputs.

In Scope:
- Typed config schema with profile blocks and validation rules.
- Deterministic merge/precedence order (CLI overrides > env > profile > defaults).
- Deterministic diagnostics for missing, invalid, and conflicting configuration.

User-Value Rationale:
Reliable configuration is required for reproducible migrations, predictable operator behavior, and low-friction adoption.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Profile selection and merge precedence produce deterministic resolved config snapshots.
- Invalid configs fail with stable, actionable diagnostics and non-zero exit semantics.
- Config resolution can be consumed cleanly by `init`/`doctor` command flows without duplicated parsing logic.

Expected Artifacts:
- Config contract/spec documenting schema, precedence, and failure semantics.
- Example config fixtures for default/dev/prod or equivalent profile classes.
- Machine-readable resolved-config artifact for CI validation.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_bootstrap/bd-n9r/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_bootstrap/bd-n9r/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for parser/validator behavior, precedence rules, and edge/failure cases.
- Integration tests covering config loading from file/env/CLI combinations.
- E2E tests validating real command execution under multiple profile scenarios.
- Structured logs capturing config source provenance, merge decisions, and validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "Implement configuration system (franken_node.toml) with profile support", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "Implement configuration system (franken_node.toml) with profile support" must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "Implement configuration system (franken_node.toml) with profile support" must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "Implement configuration system (franken_node.toml) with profile support" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "Implement configuration system (franken_node.toml) with profile support" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:29:19.182078063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:28:22.285302128Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:28:22.285275769Z","close_reason":"Completed: centralized config resolution with precedence + init/doctor integration + verification artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n9r","depends_on_id":"bd-3rp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:29:33.007345052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-nbh7","title":"[16] Contribution: benchmark/verifier methodology publications","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 16

Task Objective:
Publish methodology for benchmark and verifier design.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [16] Contribution: benchmark/verifier methodology publications are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [16] Contribution: benchmark/verifier methodology publications are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:

 {"id":"bd-nglx","title":"[11] Contract field: rollback command","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 11

Task Objective:
Require concrete rollback command/procedure for every major subsystem change.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [11] Contract field: rollback command are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [11] Contract field: rollback command are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-nglx/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-nglx/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[11] Contract field: rollback command" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[11] Contract field: rollback command" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every evidence contract includes a rollback command: a single executable command or script that reverts the change completely.
2. The rollback command must be: (a) copy-pasteable (no placeholders requiring manual substitution), (b) idempotent (safe to run multiple times), (c) tested in CI (the command is executed in a test environment and verified to restore prior state).
3. The contract must specify rollback scope: what is reverted (config, binary, data) and what is NOT reverted (e.g., already-processed events).
4. CI rejects contracts where rollback command is missing, contains unresolved placeholders, or is not tested.
5. Unit test: a contract with tested, idempotent rollback command passes; one with placeholder variables or untested command fails.
6. Rollback command execution time must be documented (estimated seconds/minutes).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyBridge","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:32.988255014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T00:45:53.242033401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T00:45:53.242005990Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-11","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nglx","depends_on_id":"bd-2ymp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:24.557214665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-nglx","author":"MistyBridge","text":"Implemented rollback-command contract field with CI enforcement and verification artifacts.

- artifacts/11/rollback_command_contract.json
- docs/templates/change_summary_template.md
- docs/change_summaries/example_change_summary.json

- .github/workflows/rollback-command-gate.yml
- artifacts/section_11/bd-nglx/{rollback_command_ci_test.json,changed_files_for_validation.txt,rollback_self_test.json,rollback_check_report.json,unittest_output.txt,rch_cargo_check.log,rch_cargo_clippy.log,rch_cargo_fmt_check.log,verification_evidence.json,verification_summary.md}

Verification:
- python3 scripts/check_rollback_command.py --self-test --json
- python3 scripts/check_rollback_command.py --changed-files artifacts/section_11/bd-nglx/changed_files_for_validation.txt --json
- python3 -m unittest tests/test_check_rollback_command.py
- rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets

@@ -135,6 +135,16 @@ def _check(name: str, ok: bool, detail: str = "") -> dict:
     return {"check": name, "passed": ok, "detail": detail or ("ok" if ok else "FAIL")}
 
 
+def _required_checks_pass(checks: list[dict], required_names: set[str]) -> bool:
+    """Fail closed when any required check is missing or failing."""
+    passed_by_name = {
+        check.get("check"): bool(check.get("passed"))
+        for check in checks
+        if isinstance(check.get("check"), str)
+    }
+    return all(passed_by_name.get(name) is True for name in required_names)
+
+
